mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142288.0 thousand as of t-1mo suggests ongoing employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no acceleration, the real interest rate is deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and driving S&P 500 returns positive over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and CPIAUCSL at 238.099 as of decision date
regime_hint: post-crisis zero-rate regime with contained inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of triggered periods, or the correlation between (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation rate) and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 1% signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447715.0 million as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly lower than non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 5.0%, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market slack that constrains profit margins and equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.0793 and UNRATE at 5.1% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with supply constraints and labor slack
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction term and forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,200 thousand units annualized, indicating strong residential investment and forward consumer wealth effects that drive equity returns positive over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on construction employment and materials demand.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1206.0 thousand as of t-2mo, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of periods where HOUST > 1200, or the average return differential versus HOUST <= 1200 periods is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.50% signals the beginning of a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.50% from current level
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of t-1mo, well below trigger
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50%, or the average return in tightening periods is not significantly lower than easing periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates below 100,000 persons monthly, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > +0.3pp AND PAYEMS monthly change < 100k
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% and PAYEMS at 142288.0 thousand as of t-1mo
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (UNRATE rise × PAYEMS deceleration) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining, indicating demand-side weakness that foreshadows earnings misses and equity multiple compression over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS 3-month change < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16324.3 billion (chained 2017 dollars) as of t-5mo, RSAFS at 447715.0 million as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction term (GDP growth × retail sales momentum) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
